description: Create a content slide on planned enhancements to BAU financial risk management capabilities using a two-column layout with narrative text and quote on the left and horizontal bar charts on the right.

header.headline: Planned enhancements to BAU financial risk management capabilities over the next 12 months
header.sub: Financial crime has become a top near-term priority for CROs, especially for G-SIBs, with growing emphasis on fraud detection, AI, and supervisory focus.

items.text: Financial crime: the renewed focus on fraud and other illegal actions – a priority for G-SIBs in particular – can also be viewed as a sign of the economic times. Two years ago, financial crime risk was not even in the top 15 of CROs’ near-term priorities; today, it’s in the top four.

CRO concerns about financial crime manifest across multiple findings. Detection of fraud and criminal activity is the most frequent use of AI within risk management operations today, and CROs expect it to be an area of increased supervisory focus in the future.
What CROs say: “People are the greater vulnerability when it comes to financial crime. It’s always your weakest link that will be the problem.”

To combat the full range of financial risks, CROs are taking an all-of-the-above approach. Enhanced stress testing and scenario planning, refinements of the risk appetite and AI deployments are all key components of financial risk management strategies.

labels: Enhanced risk measurement and stress testing or scenario analysis
Changes in risk appetite and limit frameworks
Improvements to risk identification and material risk assessment
Simplifying and modernizing risk technology and data infrastructure
Operating model efficiency or changes to accountability across first and second line of defense
Strengthening monitoring of interconnected or systemic risks, including exposures to nonbank financial institutions (NBFIs)
Simplifying frameworks and policies and/or embedding policy in technology
